ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for chicken-topped loaded fries with garlic ranch seasoning. Closest parent is french fries food product (FOODON_00002490). Chicken topping and ranch flavouring are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Chicken meat, cooked) used as closest parent for the chicken component. No composite FoodEx2 code exists for chicken-loaded fries with garlic ranch flavouring as a single dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific fast-food composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Chicken Loaded Fries Garlic Ranch Flavour is approximately 280 g, consisting of a portion of deep-fried potato fries topped with fried chicken pieces and drizzled with garlic ranch sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Fast Foods, french fries (USDA SR Legacy); Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (USDA SR Legacy); Ranch dressing (USDA SR Legacy) — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact nutritional entry exists for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries Garlic Ranch Flavour in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by weighted composite of three USDA SR Legacy entries: (1) Fast Foods, french fries (FDC ID 171937, ~55% by weight), (2) Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (FDC ID 171477, ~35% by weight), and (3) Salad dressing, ranch dressing, regular (FDC ID 172038, ~10% by weight). Proportions are based on typical fast-food loaded fries composition. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: French fries GI value — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries Garlic Ranch Flavour. GI of 63 is adopted from published GI values for french fries (deep-fried potato) from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). The chicken and ranch sauce components contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 74.2) / 100 = 46.7, rounded to 46.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1176 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single serving provides approximately 49–59% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000–2400 mg, primarily from the seasoning blend, garlic ranch sauce, and fried chicken coating.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 63) combined with large carbohydrate load per serving (~74 g) yields a high glycemic load (~47)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish as the high glycemic load per serving (~47) from deep-fried potato fries can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods or fibre-rich vegetables is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (14.8 g/100 g), saturated fat (3.2 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and sodium (~420 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in total fat, saturated fat, and sodium, and contains trace trans fats from deep-frying, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Loaded Fries Garlic Ranch Flavour is a fast-food dish served at Jollibee outlets in Singapore, consisting of deep-fried potato fries topped with pieces of battered and fried chicken, finished with a garlic ranch-flavoured sauce or seasoning. The dish is ultra-processed and combines the crispy, starchy texture of deep-fried fries with the savoury, juicy character of fried chicken and the creamy, tangy, garlicky notes of ranch dressing. It is sold as a snack or side dish and is tagged as Halal-certified at the Jollibee Bugis Village outlet. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 268 kcal per 100 g, with a significant fat content (~14.8 g/100 g) and moderate protein (~10.2 g/100 g) from the chicken component. The carbohydrate content (~26.5 g/100 g) is predominantly from the potato fries, which carry a medium glycemic index of approximately 63, resulting in a high glycemic load per standard serving (~46.6). Sodium content is elevated (~420 mg/100 g) due to the seasoning blend and ranch sauce, making this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. It is best consumed occasionally and in moderation as part of a balanced diet.

Is Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries Halal?
How much sodium is in Chicken Loaded Fries Garlic Ranch?
Is Chicken Loaded Fries suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of loaded fries?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific Jollibee menu item exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for french fries, fried battered chicken, and ranch dressing. GI is adopted from the closest analogous food (french fries) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy data for french fries (~55% weight), fried battered chicken (~35% weight), and ranch dressing (~10% weight); no exact match was found in any approved primary source. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. Trans fat is estimated at 0.2 g/100 g based on typical deep-frying conditions; this value should be verified if an official product specification becomes available. Glycemic index of 63 is an analogous estimate from published french fries GI data (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., 2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated using 280 g serving size: (63 × 74.2 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 46.7, reported as 46.6. Halal status confirmed from field survey cuisine tags for Jollibee Bugis Village. Allergen profile (gluten, milk, egg, soy) is typical for this dish type but should be verified against Jollibee Singapore's official allergen declaration. Multilingual names are translations of the canonical English dish name; no official local-language branding was found for this specific menu item. All -1 values in per_100g indicate nutrients not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient estimate from three USDA SR Legacy sources with documented weight proportions.
Adopted GI 63 from University of Sydney GI Database (french fries analogue) with full documentation.
Added mandatory multilingual names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes field, specifically calling out vitamin_d_mcg.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency.
Set ontology match types to only allowed values: closest_parent or none.
Included zero-macro justification rule check — no core macros are zero, so no justification phrase required.
Set human_condition_name to 'Type 2 Diabetes' for the type2_diabetes key.
